module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Module Project: Start Volunteer Hub History
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: Begin the cumulative volunteer-hub project and record its first snapshot.

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Inspect the Volunteer Hub Folder
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt: Type `pwd` and press Enter. Then type `ls` and press Enter. Confirm you are inside `volunteer-hub/` and can see README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/. Do not initialize Git yet.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: Inspect the prepared folder before changing it.

help.concept: A repository begins as an ordinary project folder.
help.hint_1: The terminal already opens inside volunteer-hub.
help.hint_2: This step must leave the folder uninitialized.

try_git_repo_inspection.starterCode:
# Volunteer Hub

Volunteer signups and schedules.
try_git_repo_inspection.instructions: Type `pwd` and press Enter. Then type `ls` and press Enter. Confirm you are inside `volunteer-hub/` and can see README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/. Do not initialize Git yet.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- volunteer-hub mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s '# Volunteer Hub Volunteer signups and schedules. ' > volunteer-hub/README.md mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s ' Volunteer Hub

Volunteer Signups

' > volunteer-hub/index.html mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > volunteer-hub/styles.css mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub/notes printf %s 'Orientation: Saturday 10 AM ' > volunteer-hub/notes/schedule.txt

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- volunteer-hub mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s '# Volunteer Hub Volunteer signups and schedules. ' > volunteer-hub/README.md mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s ' Volunteer Hub

Volunteer Signups

' > volunteer-hub/index.html mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > volunteer-hub/styles.css mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub/notes printf %s 'Orientation: Saturday 10 AM ' > volunteer-hub/notes/schedule.txt

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_git_config.title: Initialize Volunteer Hub
try_git_config.prompt: Type `git init -b main` and press Enter inside `volunteer-hub/`.
try_git_config.hint: Initialize the current folder on main.

help.concept: The parallel project uses the same initialization skill in a different repository.

try_git_config.starterCode:
# Volunteer Hub

Volunteer signups and schedules.
try_git_config.instructions: Type `git init -b main` and press Enter inside `volunteer-hub/`.

try_git_init.title: Configure the Volunteer Hub Identity
try_git_init.prompt: Type `git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"` and press Enter. Then type `git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"` and press Enter.

help.concept: Each independent repository needs author identity before its first commit.

try_git_init.starterCode:
# Volunteer Hub

Volunteer signups and schedules.
try_git_init.solutionCode:
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
try_git_init.instructions: Type `git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"` and press Enter. Then type `git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"` and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- volunteer-hub mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub git -C volunteer-hub init -q -b main mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s '# Volunteer Hub Volunteer signups and schedules. ' > volunteer-hub/README.md mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s ' Volunteer Hub

Volunteer Signups

' > volunteer-hub/index.html mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > volunteer-hub/styles.css mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub/notes printf %s 'Orientation: Saturday 10 AM ' > volunteer-hub/notes/schedule.txt

Volunteer Signups

' > volunteer-hub/index.html mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > volunteer-hub/styles.css mkdir -p -- volunteer-hub/notes printf %s 'Orientation: Saturday 10 AM ' > volunteer-hub/notes/schedule.txt

Did you run `git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"`?

try_git_stage_files.title: Stage the Volunteer Hub Baseline
try_git_stage_files.prompt: Type `git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/schedule.txt` and press Enter. Then type `git diff --staged --stat` and press Enter.
try_git_stage_files.hint: Stage the four authored files and inspect the proposed snapshot.

help.concept: The module project reapplies staging without repeating the full lesson explanation.
help.hint_2: Do not stage hidden platform files.

try_git_stage_files.starterCode:
# Volunteer Hub

Volunteer signups and schedules.
try_git_stage_files.solutionCode:
git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/schedule.txt
git diff --staged --stat
try_git_stage_files.instructions: Type `git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/schedule.txt` and press Enter. Then type `git diff --staged --stat` and press Enter.

try_git_commit.title: Commit the Volunteer Hub Baseline
try_git_commit.prompt: Type `git commit -m "Start volunteer hub"` and press Enter. Then type `git log --oneline` and press Enter. Type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_git_commit.hint: Record the staged baseline with the exact message and verify it.

help.concept: The parallel repository now has the canonical state used by the next module project.
help.hint_2: The final short status should be empty.

try_git_commit.starterCode:
# Volunteer Hub

Volunteer signups and schedules.
try_git_commit.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Start volunteer hub"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_git_commit.instructions: Type `git commit -m "Start volunteer hub"` and press Enter. Then type `git log --oneline` and press Enter. Type `git status --short` and press Enter.

moduleProject.title: Build the Volunteer Hub Baseline
moduleProject.description: This project starts a second cumulative repository. It uses different files from the guided community-site work but requires the same Module 1 outcomes: inspect, initialize, configure, stage, commit, and verify. The final state continues into the Module 2 project.

intro-to-git-repository.title: Parallel Project: Volunteer Hub
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown: This project starts a second cumulative repository. It uses different files from the guided community-site work but requires the same Module 1 outcomes: inspect, initialize, configure, stage, commit, and verify. The final state continues into the Module 2 project.
